openmeter/portal/adapter.purpose: JWT-backed stateless implementation of portal.Service that issues and validates short-lived HS256 tokens scoped to namespace, subject, and optional meter slug allowlist. ListTokens and InvalidateToken are intentionally unimplemented stubs returning GenericNotImplementedError — no database backing exists by design.

patterns.name: Config-validated constructor
patterns.description: New(Config) validates all fields via Config.Validate() before constructing the adapter; always return (portal.Service, error) — never panic on bad config.
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (portal.Service, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err } ... }

patterns.name: Interface compliance assertion
patterns.description: var _ portal.Service = (*adapter)(nil) at package level — compile-time proof the struct satisfies the interface.

patterns.name: JWT claims struct embeds jwt.RegisteredClaims
patterns.description: JTWPortalTokenClaims embeds jwt.RegisteredClaims and adds domain fields (Namespace, Id, AllowedMeterSlugs). Always parse with ParseWithClaims into this type, never into map[string]interface{}.
patterns.example: type JTWPortalTokenClaims struct { jwt.RegisteredClaims; Namespace string; AllowedMeterSlugs []string }

patterns.name: Strict JWT parse options — never relax
patterns.description: Validate uses WithStrictDecoding, WithExpirationRequired, WithIssuer, and WithValidMethods. Do not remove any of these options when extending validation.

patterns.name: Noop adapter for disabled/test contexts
patterns.description: NewNoop() returns a full no-op portal.Service where every method returns models.NewGenericNotImplementedError — not nil error. Use in Wire when portal is disabled.
patterns.example: func (a *noopAdapter) CreateToken(ctx context.Context, input portal.CreateTokenInput) (*portal.PortalToken, error) { return nil, models.NewGenericNotImplementedError(fmt.Errorf("noop adapter")) }

key_file_guides.role: Config struct, Validate(), New() constructor, adapter struct definition
key_file_guides.watch_for: Secret must be []byte internally; Config.Secret is string — conversion happens in New(). Expire=0 is rejected by Validate(). Always call New() and check the returned error.

key_file_guides.role: CreateToken (JWT sign) and Validate (JWT parse + claims extraction); ListTokens and InvalidateToken stub out with NotImplemented
key_file_guides.watch_for: Token field is intentionally NOT populated in toAPIPortalToken — the raw token string is only exposed in CreateToken response. Adding it elsewhere is a security risk.

key_file_guides.role: Full no-op portal.Service for tests and disabled-portal wiring
key_file_guides.watch_for: All methods must return models.NewGenericNotImplementedError, not nil error — callers distinguish 'not implemented' from success. Never add business logic here.

anti_patterns: Storing token state in a database here — this adapter is intentionally stateless; persistence belongs in a future DB-backed adapter
Relaxing JWT validation options (removing WithExpirationRequired, WithStrictDecoding, WithValidMethods, or WithIssuer)
Populating the Token field inside toAPIPortalToken — token string must only appear in CreateToken response
Calling New() without checking the returned error
Adding business logic to the noop adapter — it must remain a pure stub returning NotImplementedError

decisions.decision: Stateless JWT tokens with no DB persistence for ListTokens/InvalidateToken
decisions.rationale: Short-lived portal tokens for metering access do not require revocation in the current design; unimplemented methods return NotImplemented to make gaps explicit rather than silently succeeding.

decisions.decision: HS256 with a shared secret rather than asymmetric signing
decisions.rationale: Portal tokens are validated by the same server that issues them, so symmetric signing is sufficient and avoids key distribution complexity.

code_examples.scenario: Creating and validating a portal token

openmeter/portal/authenticator.purpose: Chi middleware that authenticates HTTP requests against v1 OpenAPI security requirements, dispatching to verifyPortalToken for PortalTokenAuth-scoped routes and injecting the validated subject into context via a typed context key.

patterns.name: OpenAPI-driven security dispatch
patterns.description: getSecurityRequirements reads per-operation security from the swagger spec, falling back to global; validateSecurityRequirements iterates alternatives and returns on first success — mirrors OpenAPI OR semantics.
patterns.example: for _, sr := range securityRequirements { r, err = a.validateSecurityRequirement(sr, w, r); if err == nil { return r, nil } }

patterns.name: Subject injected via typed context key
patterns.description: Authenticated subject is stored under AuthenticatorSubjectSessionKey (typed AuthenticatorContextKey string). Retrieve with GetAuthenticatedSubject(ctx) — never use raw string key.

patterns.name: Security scheme name matched by prefix split
patterns.description: getAuthenticatorFunc splits api.PortalTokenAuthScopes on '.' and matches by first segment. Adding a new auth scheme requires a new case in this switch.

patterns.name: Nil security requirement means unauthenticated route
patterns.description: If getSecurityRequirements returns nil (no matching route/operation), the middleware calls next directly without authentication.
patterns.example: if sr == nil { next.ServeHTTP(w, r); return }

key_file_guides.role: Entire authenticator: struct, constructor, Chi middleware factory, portal token verification, OpenAPI security resolution
key_file_guides.watch_for: verifyPortalToken extracts meterSlug from chi.URLParam — if the route param name changes this check silently stops enforcing slug allowlists. AllowedMeterSlugs check uses slices.Contains; empty slice means all slugs allowed. Auth failures must always be 401 via models.NewStatusProblem.

anti_patterns: Adding new security schemes by hardcoding names as plain strings instead of using api.* constants split on '.'
Calling portal.Validate outside verifyPortalToken — token validation must flow through the middleware, not ad hoc
Skipping the AllowedMeterSlugs check when meterSlug is empty or the route has no slug param
Returning 200 or 500 for auth failures — unauthorized must always be 401 via models.NewStatusProblem

decisions.decision: Security requirements read from swagger spec at request time rather than compiled into route handlers
decisions.rationale: Keeps auth policy co-located with the OpenAPI spec; new endpoints automatically inherit security without changing middleware code.

openmeter/portal/httphandler.purpose: v1 HTTP handler package (package httpdriver) for portal token endpoints — implements the httptransport.Handler pattern for CreateToken, ListTokens, and InvalidateToken, delegating to portal.Service and meter.Service for cross-domain meter slug validation.

patterns.name: Composite Handler interface with sub-interfaces
patterns.description: Top-level Handler embeds TokenHandler sub-interface; each operation returns a typed handler alias. var _ Handler = (*handler)(nil) enforces compile-time compliance.
patterns.example:
type Handler interface { TokenHandler }
type TokenHandler interface { CreateToken() CreateTokenHandler; ListTokens() ListTokensHandler; InvalidateToken() InvalidateTokenHandler }

patterns.name: httptransport.NewHandler / NewHandlerWithArgs per endpoint
patterns.description: Each endpoint is a closure pair: decoder func + operation func + ResponseEncoder + options. Params-bearing endpoints use NewHandlerWithArgs; param-less use NewHandler.

patterns.name: Namespace resolved at the start of every decoder
patterns.description: resolveNamespace(ctx) wraps namespaceDecoder.GetNamespace; returns HTTP 500 on failure. Always call this at the very start of every decoder func before any other logic.
patterns.example:
ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx)
if err != nil { return ..., err }

patterns.name: Domain input type as request type alias
patterns.description: Request types reuse domain input structs directly (CreateTokenRequest = portal.CreateTokenInput) rather than duplicating fields, keeping the handler thin.

patterns.name: Token field added post-mapping in CreateToken operation only
patterns.description: toAPIPortalToken deliberately omits the raw JWT token string; CreateToken operation manually sets portalToken.Token = token.Token after calling the mapping helper.

patterns.name: WithOperationName appended on every handler
patterns.description: httptransport.AppendOptions always appends WithOperationName (e.g. 'createPortalToken') for OTel span naming — never omit this.

key_file_guides.role: Handler/TokenHandler interfaces, handler struct, New() constructor, resolveNamespace helper
key_file_guides.watch_for: New() takes variadic HandlerOption — callers pass error handler and tracer options here; forgetting them silences errors and breaks OTel tracing.

key_file_guides.role: All three endpoint implementations: ListTokens, CreateToken, InvalidateToken
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreateToken validates AllowedMeterSlugs against live meter list via meterService.ListMeters in the operation phase, not decode phase. InvalidateToken returns 204 (EmptyResponseEncoder), not 200.

key_file_guides.role: toAPIPortalToken maps portal.PortalToken to api.PortalToken; sets Expired flag if ExpiresAt is in the past
key_file_guides.watch_for: Token field is intentionally NOT mapped here — always set it explicitly in CreateToken after calling toAPIPortalToken. Adding it here would be a security risk.

anti_patterns: Returning the raw JWT token string via toAPIPortalToken — it must be set manually only in CreateToken operation
Adding database calls directly in handlers — delegate to portal.Service or meter.Service
Omitting WithOperationName from AppendOptions — breaks OTel tracing span names
Using context.Background() instead of the request context in decoder or operation closures

decisions.decision: Meter slug validation in CreateToken operation rather than inside portal.Service
decisions.rationale: portal.Service is JWT-only and has no knowledge of meters; cross-domain validation belongs in the HTTP handler layer which has access to both services.

decisions.decision: Request type aliases to domain input structs
decisions.rationale: Avoids duplicating field definitions; keeps the handler thin and the domain type as the single source of truth for input shape.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new portal endpoint following the existing handler pattern
